collection.title: The Collection
collection.subtitle: Six original compositions. Each a meditation. Each a gift worthy of the subject it portrays.

product.description: A rendering of profound stillness — the shepherd who leaves the ninety-nine. This composition draws from the Flemish tradition, rendered in the warm ochres and umbers of old master painting. Printed on 300 GSM archival fine art paper and hand-assembled into your chosen frame in the United States.
product.ships_from: Ships from the United States · Lifetime Warranty
product.edition_note: Edition of 500 · Certificate of authenticity included

about.hero_quote: We believe in the transformative power of sacred art — not as decoration, but as devotion.
about.section1_body:
There is a long tradition of sacred portraiture — from the Byzantine icon to the Flemish masters — in which artists gave everything to the rendering of the divine. We believe that tradition deserves to live in homes today.

Veritas was founded to make that possible. Not devotional kitsch. Not mass-produced sentiment. But genuine, considered sacred art, made to the standard of the homes it will occupy.
about.section2_body:
Every composition begins with deep study — of scripture, of art history, of the theological tradition the piece will enter. We work with artists who understand that a portrait of Christ is not a product. It is a responsibility.

Each piece is printed on 300 GSM archival fine art paper using UV-resistant inks, then hand-assembled into frames built in the United States. The result holds its integrity — visually and structurally — for generations.
about.section3_body: We offer a lifetime warranty on every frame and print we ship. We ship fully assembled, with all hardware included. We stand behind our work because we believe the homes and families who welcome it deserve nothing less.
about.closing_quote: Truth, beautifully rendered, needs no defense. It only requires a frame worthy of its weight.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: All prints are produced on 300 GSM archival fine art paper using UV-resistant, pigment-based inks. Frames are solid wood, hand-assembled in the United States. We do not use MDF or composite framing materials.
faq.a2: Each piece is assembled to order. Please allow 7–10 business days for production, plus shipping time. Expedited options are available at checkout.
faq.a3: All sales are final. Because each piece is produced to order, we are unable to accept returns. If your order arrives damaged, please contact us within 48 hours and we will make it right.
faq.a4: Orders may be cancelled within 24 hours of placement. After that window, production has begun and cancellations cannot be accommodated.
faq.a5: We offer three standard sizes: Small (11×14"), Medium (16×20"), and Large (24×36"). Custom sizing is available on request — please contact us before ordering.
faq.a6: We currently offer five frame finishes: Matte Black, Walnut, Antique White, Burnished Gold, and Natural Oak. All frames are solid wood with a UV-protective acrylic face.
faq.a7: We currently ship within the United States only. International availability is planned for later this year. Sign up for our list to be notified.
faq.a8: Yes. We offer a lifetime warranty on all frame and print defects. Normal wear is not covered, but manufacturing defects are covered forever. No receipt required — your order number is sufficient.
faq.q9: Can I request a custom size?
faq.a9: Yes. Custom sizing is available for most compositions. Please reach out before placing your order so we can confirm availability and provide a custom quote.

policies.title: Policies
policies.returns_body:
All Veritas pieces are produced to order. Because each print is assembled specifically for you, we are unable to accept returns or exchanges.

If your order arrives damaged or defective, contact us within 48 hours of delivery with photographs of the damage. We will arrange a replacement at no cost.

Orders may be cancelled within 24 hours of placement. After that window, production has begun and the order cannot be cancelled. To request a cancellation within the 24-hour window, email us immediately with your order number.
policies.shipping_body:
Veritas ships within the United States only at this time. All orders are shipped fully assembled and ready to hang, carefully packaged to protect the frame in transit.

Production takes 7–10 business days. Shipping typically takes an additional 3–5 business days via ground service. Expedited shipping is available at checkout.

You will receive a tracking number when your order ships. We are not responsible for delays caused by carriers.
policies.warranty_body:
Every Veritas frame and print is covered by a lifetime warranty against manufacturing defects. This includes:

• Frame structural defects
• Print fading or color shift under normal indoor conditions
• Mounting hardware failure

This warranty does not cover damage from mishandling, moisture, direct sunlight exposure, or normal wear. To make a warranty claim, contact us with your order number. No receipt required.
policies.privacy_body:
Veritas collects only the information necessary to process your order: your name, shipping address, and email. We do not sell, rent, or share your personal information with third parties.

We use your email address to send order confirmations and shipping updates. You may opt out of marketing communications at any time.

Our website uses standard analytics to understand how visitors use the site. This data is anonymous and not linked to personal information.

For any privacy concerns, contact us at privacy@veritasart.com.

footer.tagline: Sacred art for the modern home.
footer.made_in: Hand assembled in the United States.
